titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/178

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=465 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:46:54.03281+00 | translation moa=466 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:46:55.519153+00

p.cl:
Sciant presentes et futuri quod ego Robertus de Bernam dedi . concessi et Hac pre
senti carta mea confirmaui deo et beate Marie de Melros et monachis ibidem deo
seruientibus in liberam et perpetuam elemosinam illud Rete in Berwycestrem' super twed’
quod Magister Dauid frater meus tunc camerarius domini Regis emit de Radulfo de Hauuill’
falconario domini Regis . et dedit illud Rogero fratri meo et heredibus suis . uel suis assig
natis De quo quidem reti idem Rogerus dotauit christianam filiam Ade marescalli uxo
rem suam in uita sua . post cuius decessum idem rete iure hereditario me con
tingit . Tenendum et habendum dictis monachis inperpetuum adeo libere et quiete . plene
et honorifice  sicut carta predicti Radulfi de Hauuil' dicto magistro dauid inde ple
nius confecta  testatur . Ego uero et heredes mei dictum rete dictis monachis omnino
ut dictum est contra omnes warantizabimus ac defendemus .

In cuius rei testi
monium ego pro me et heredibus meis huic scripto sigillum meum apponi fe
ci .

Let present and future know that I, Robert de Bernham, have given, granted and by this my present charter made firm to God and Blessed Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place, in free and everlasting alms, the net in Berwickstream upon the Tweed, which Master David my brother, then chamberlain of the lord king, purchased from Ralph de Hauville, falconer of the lord king, and gave it to Roger my brother and to his heirs or his assignees. From this net the same Roger endowed Christiana daughter of Adam the Marshal, his wife, for her lifetime, after whose death the net shall belong to me by reight of heritage. For the said monks to hold and have as freely and quitely, fully and honourably, as the charter of the said Ralph de Hauville, made more fully thereupon for the said Master David, bears witness. I and my heirs shall entirely warrant and defend the said net to the said monks against all people.
In testimony of this matter I, for myself and for my heirs, have applied my seal to this written deed.
As witness, the community of Berwick.
